rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, July 26, 2017 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant The W.W. Henry Company, L.P.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that decedent Walter Zabel was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. The deposition testimony of Larry Lay and plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Additionally, the limited questioning of Mr. Lay fails to demonstrate that he does not know and cannot remember the number designation for the Henry's wet patch product at issue. Defendant also failed to sustain its burden of presenting evidence to negate an essential element of plaintiffs' claims. The declaration of Lori Angelo is insufficient to demonstrate that the Henry's wet patch product at issue was more likely than not a non-asbestos-containing product. Plaintiffs' objections to the declaration of Lori Angelo are overruled. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: ERNESTINE BROWN ET AL VS. INGERSOLL-RAND COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, July 26, 2017 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Albay Construction Company's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that defendant's employees did not work in the presence of decedent Paul Brown. The declaration of Gary Miles is insufficient to do so. When read in conjunction with the deposition testimony of Monte Manwill, Mr. Miles' declaration merely creates a triable issue of material fact whether Albay employees worked in the presence of decedent, the sole issue raised by defendant's motion. Plaintiffs' objections to the declaration of Gary Miles are overruled. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)
